commit.message:
Wire transaction tracking for all contract mutations

All four mutation operations (create, check-in, withdraw, claim)
now register their transactions with TransactionContext. This
enables live status tracking in the TransactionTracker UI.

Changes:
- Modified contractService methods to return txId from onFinish
- Updated useHabits to call addTransaction for each mutation
- Imported and wired useTransactions hook

The TransactionTracker component will now show pending, confirmed,
and failed statuses for all submitted transactions.

commit.message:
Reset loading state when wallet picker is cancelled

The wallet connection loading spinner would freeze if a user
dismissed the picker modal without selecting a wallet. This
occurred because showConnect has no onCancel callback by default,
leaving isLoading stuck at true.

Added an onCancel handler to walletService.connect that resets
the loading state when the picker is dismissed.

commit.message:
Add optimistic update for bonus claim mutation

When a user claims a bonus, immediately update the UI to reflect
the claimed state. This prevents the 'Claim Bonus' button from
staying visible after a successful claim, which could lead to users
attempting duplicate claims that would fail on-chain.

The optimistic update sets bonusClaimed: true and is rolled back
if the transaction fails.

commit.message:
feat: add support for ai_toolchain_operator_enabled (#741)

Add the ai_toolchain_operator_enabled variable to enable the AI Toolchain
Operator (Kaito) on AKS clusters. This is a simple top-level boolean
attribute (defaults to false) that allows users to declaratively enable
KAITO for AI/ML inference workload management.

Changes:
- variables.tf: Add ai_toolchain_operator_enabled variable (bool, default false)
- main.tf: Pass the variable to azurerm_kubernetes_cluster resource
- versions.tf: Bump azurerm minimum version to >= 4.48.0 (required for this attribute)

Closes #737

Add Python FastAPI cloud backend for Minecraft anticheat system

- Tier 2: Statistical pattern detection (PatternDetector) using numpy/scipy
  for click CPS/entropy, movement speed/fly, combat reach/aimbot heuristics

  aim_analysis, and movement_analysis sub-modules with Redis caching

  (signs body+timestamp to match Java CloudConnector convention)
- GET /players/{uuid}, /players/{uuid}/analyses, /players/search

- BehavioralFingerprintService: Gaussian similarity comparison for alt-account detection
- CrossServerService: Redis-backed activity tracking, violation aggregation,
  ban evasion detection via fingerprint similarity
- SQLAlchemy async models: Player, Analysis
- Pydantic v2 schemas with UUID format validation
- Docker + docker-compose with PostgreSQL 16 + Redis 7

commit.message:
Server-based CD: ACR polling + ring auto-promotion (#1439) (#1521)

* Server-based CD: ACR polling + ring auto-promotion

ReleasePollingService:
- Polls ACR v2 tags/list API for new image tags (configurable interval)
- Auto-detects latest tag by version score (YYYYMMDD * 100000 + BUILD)
- When new tag found, promotes canary-ring targets: DesiredImageTag + Status=Upgrading
- Config: ReleasePolling.Enabled, AcrLoginServer, Repository, TagSuffix, PollSeconds
- Disabled by default (ReleasePolling.Enabled=false)

RingPromotionService:
- Checks every 5 minutes for ring auto-promotion eligibility
- Ring chain via PredecessorRingSlug (canary → early → production)
- Requires ALL predecessor-ring targets to be Active + Healthy
- Respects MonitoringWindowMinutes before promoting
- Only promotes rings with AutoPromote=true

Combined with existing DeploymentWatcherService, this gives a fully
server-based CD pipeline: ACR poll → canary deploy → monitor → promote.

Closes #1439

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove auto-promotion services — CD is manual via UI

CI deploys canary + runs tests. Canary sends heartbeats with its
version to the control plane. Operator sees canary version in the
Instance Heartbeats entity, sets DesiredImageTag on production
targets, clicks Upgrade. DeploymentWatcherService handles kubectl.

No auto-promotion — operator controls ring progression manually.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 4 evaluation bugs in SKILL.md

1. Wrong grading.json schema: replace assertions/with_skill/without_skill
   wrapper with the correct flat schema (expectations, summary.pass_rate)
   that grader.md actually produces and aggregate_benchmark.py reads.

2. Missing run-1/ layer: output paths lacked the run-* subdirectory
   required by aggregate_benchmark.py, which skips any config dir with
   no run-* children. Updated with_skill and without_skill paths to
   include run-1/.

3. Trigger eval format and copy step: Section 14 Step 2 never showed
   the {query, should_trigger} array format that run_loop.py requires,
   and had no step to move ~/Downloads/eval_set.json to the workspace
   path used in the run_loop command.

4. Ambiguous grader.md path: instruction now says to pass the absolute
   path <skill-dir>/agents/grader.md when spawning the grader subagent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(responses): async LRO mode -- 201 Accepted for non-blocking agent comms (#47)

* feat(responses): async LRO mode — 201 Accepted for non-blocking agent comms

When async: true is set in the request body, POST /v1/responses returns
201 { id, status: accepted, created_at } immediately and runs sendAndWait
in the background. Without the flag, behavior is identical to before.

This is the foundation for issue #38 — eliminates cascade deadlocks in
multi-agent fleets by decoupling the HTTP response from LLM processing.

- Add buildAcceptedResponse() helper in responses.mjs
- Add async branch in handleResponses() before streaming/sync paths
- Hoist timeout extraction to share across async and sync paths
- 5 new tests (201 envelope, background call, timeout, validation, compat)
- Bump responses extension 0.3.0 -> 0.4.0

Closes #38 (Task 1)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add async LRO mode documentation

- README.md: async request format, 201 response, curl example, architecture
  diagrams for sync vs async flows, note on future result delivery
- extension-responses.md: async mode section with inter-agent use case

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: bump registry version 0.19.0 -> 0.20.0

commit.message:
Add 10 coding category SKILL.md files

Creates complete, detailed SKILL.md files for the coding category:

- code-reviewer: Review checklist with severity levels (critical/major/minor/nit)
- debugger: Systematic reproduce→isolate→hypothesize→test→fix methodology
- refactorer: Refactoring patterns (Extract Function, DRY, SOLID, Guard Clauses)
- api-designer: RESTful design, OpenAPI snippets, pagination, versioning
- test-writer: Test pyramid, AAA pattern, mocking, Jest and Supertest examples
- sql-expert: Query optimization, window functions, CTEs, N+1 anti-pattern
- regex-master: Character classes, quantifiers, groups, common pattern library
- security-auditor: OWASP Top 10, SQL injection, XSS, auth flaw examples
- architecture-designer: Pattern trade-offs, ADR template, system design examples
- documentation-writer: README structure, JSDoc/Python/GoDoc docstrings, CHANGELOG

Each file includes frontmatter, Overview, When to Use/Not, Quick Reference
table, numbered Instructions, 2+ code examples, Best Practices, Common
Mistakes, Tips & Tricks, and Related Skills cross-links.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
